title: Reasoning, Planning, Decomposition, and Verification · Evaluation Brief: Decomposition
summary: Prompt a difficult migration without exposing or demanding hidden reasoning. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt decomposition increment in a plan-execute-verify task contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Decompose work around independently verifiable outcomes and dependency order.
Use planning before implementation when scope, dependencies, or user choices are materially uncertain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Breaking a task into the most files produces the best plan. This fails the Decomposition contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Decomposition evidence
